title: Monster Mouths Sheet
bio: Chompkin Doodlejaw is a mouth practice goblin who keeps tiny teeth in a teacup. He feels dramatic, gargles moonlight, and gives every frown a snack-sized story.

steps.title: Base Shapes
steps.instruction: On the Guides layer, draw a page rectangle. Add six small rounded rectangles or ovals where the monster mouths will sit.
steps.hint: Think of each mouth as a tiny window for teeth, tongues, or giggles.

steps.title: Face Placement
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, draw different mouth shapes: grin, tiny frown, zigzag teeth, open oval, side smirk, and wobbly nervous smile.
steps.hint: Change the mouth curve first, then add teeth after.

steps.title: Personality Details
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, add simple teeth triangles, tiny tongues, drool dots, labels, cheek marks, and a few crumb-sized sparkles.
steps.hint: Keep teeth rounded at the tips so the mouths stay cute-creepy.

steps.title: Clean Lineart
steps.instruction: On the Lineart layer, trace each mouth, tooth, tongue, label, cheek mark, and sparkle with clear careful lines.
steps.hint: Trace the outside mouth shape first, then the small details inside.

steps.title: Color & Details
steps.instruction: On the Color + Details layer, color tongues, cheeks, teeth shadows, and tiny accents. Add a soft page shadow below.
steps.hint: A little dark color inside open mouths makes the expressions easier to read.
